name: ExtensionTestScript_Identifier
title: R5: identifier
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `TestScript.identifier` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`TestScript.identifier` 0..* `Identifier`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `TestScript.identifier` 0..* `Identifier`
*  R4B: `TestScript.identifier` 0..1 `Identifier`
*  R4: `TestScript.identifier` 0..1 `Identifier`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `TestScript.identifier` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `TestScript.identifier` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `TestScript.identifier` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.comment:
Element `TestScript.identifier` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `TestScript.identifier` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `TestScript.identifier` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
Typically, this is used for identifiers that can go in an HL7 V3 II (instance identifier) data type, and can then identify this test script outside of FHIR, where it is not possible to use the logical URI.

element.short: Additional identifier for the test script
element.definition: A formal identifier that is used to identify this test script when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.
element.comment: Typically, this is used for identifiers that can go in an HL7 V3 II (instance identifier) data type, and can then identify this test script outside of FHIR, where it is not possible to use the logical URI.
